name: TensorBoard
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); TensorBoard.dev (free cloud hosting for public experiments)

auth.notes: Local: no auth; TensorBoard.dev cloud requires Google account

performance.notes: Local file-based; UI latency depends on log size; large runs can be slow to load

agent_readiness.notes: Google's ML training visualization tool. Logs scalars (loss, accuracy), histograms, images, embeddings, computational graphs. Built into TensorFlow; PyTorch support via torch.utils.tensorboard. Works with PyTorch Lightning, Keras, JAX via SummaryWriter. No REST API — file-based log format (tfevents). UI for visualization only; no programmatic query API.

gotchas: No REST API for programmatic metric querying — file-based only
Not designed for experiment comparison across many runs (Weights & Biases better)
UI is dated compared to modern alternatives (W&B, Neptune)
PyTorch integration works but TensorFlow is first-class
best_when: TensorFlow or Keras projects needing native built-in visualization
Simple single-run training monitoring without cloud connectivity
GPU utilization and profiling via TensorBoard Profiler
avoid_when: Multi-run experiment comparison and collaboration (use W&B or MLflow)
Programmatic metric access from agents (no REST API)
Teams needing cloud-based experiment storage and sharing
summary: TensorBoard is Google's ML training visualization tool with built-in TensorFlow integration and PyTorch support. Logs scalars, histograms, images, and embeddings to local tfevents files. File-based (no REST API). Best for simple training visualization, especially in TensorFlow/Keras projects. For experiment comparison, collaboration, or programmatic access, use Weights & Biases or MLflow.
